Example: To get URLs from items like [{"metadata":{"url":"example.com"}}], use flatten="metadata" and then fields="metadata.url". The flattening transforms nested objects into dot-notation format (e.g. {"metadata":{"url":"x"}} becomes {"metadata.url":"x"}). Retrieve only the fields you need, reducing the response size and improving performance. The response includes total count, offset, limit, and items array. Arguments: - datasetId: Dataset ID or username~dataset-name. (required) - clean: If true, returns only non-empty items and skips hidden fields (starting with #). No limit by default. - fields: Comma-separated list of fields to include in results. Fields in output are sorted as specified. For nested objects, use dot notation (e.g. "metadata.url") after flattening. - omit: Comma-separated list of fields to exclude from results. - desc: If true, results are returned in reverse order (newest to oldest). - flatten: Comma-separated list of fields which should transform nested objects into flat structures.
